title: gamopetalae
intro: Gamopetalae is a taxonomical rank that refers to a group of flowering plants with fused petals, forming a single unit. This characteristic is commonly seen in various plant families, such as the orchid family (Orchidaceae) with its intricate and showy flowers, the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae) with its often solitary and waxy blooms, and the sedge family (Cyperaceae) with its simple and usually greenish flowers. The gamopetalae group encompasses a wide range of flowering plant families, each with unique characteristics and adaptations. By grouping these plants based on their shared petal fusion, botanists can better understand their evolutionary relationships and similarities.

families.family_desc: Hamamelidaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the witch-hazel family, which comprises about 100 species of deciduous shrubs and small trees.
